page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-48GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration delivers 8 shared vCPUs across 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, paired with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores fall within the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for cached, small-block reads at 830.42 GB/sec, but uncached large-block read and write speeds drop to the bottom 25% weak category at 36.84 GB/sec and 17.27 GB/sec, respectively.

The shared CPU allocation presents a resource-density tradeoff, offering a cost-efficient profile for workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance. This server is suited for multi-threaded tasks such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, where the 48.0 GB memory capacity can be leveraged alongside average multi-core processing. However, it is less suitable for single-threaded applications or memory-intensive tasks requiring high uncached memory throughput.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It provides 8 shared vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 48.0 GB of system memory, and no bundled local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, falling in the bottom 10%, while multi-core CPU and Geekbench scores reside in the average middle 50% range. Uncached memory bandwidth is also limited, scoring in the bottom 25%. Due to its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-efficient option for workloads that benefit from higher memory capacity but do not demand sustained, dedicated single-core processing. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 KVM virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 48.0 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A shared-core virtual server combining moderate multi-threaded processing with a balanced memory footprint for cost-conscious general-purpose workloads.

KVM hypervisor virtualization on x86_64 architecture
